title: The Full Stack Recap
concept: Prompting, context engineering, embeddings, chunking, vector search, retrieval, reranking, RAG, and MCP as one coherent stack.

exercises.prompt: Before connecting databases, what is the core technique of designing specific text inputs to guide an LLM's response style and optimizing text inputs to guide LLM behavior?
exercises.explain: Prompt engineering is the foundation of controlling LLMs, allowing us to guide their behavior and format their outputs.

cards.title: Putting the Puzzle Together
cards.body: We've built chunks, vector databases, and custom prompts. Now, it's time to assemble these individual parts into a unified, production-grade engine that connects LLMs to real-time tools and documents.

cards.title: The LLM Application Stack
cards.body: An LLM Application Stack combines chunking, embeddings, vector search, reranking, and protocols like MCP (Model Context Protocol) to feed LLMs the exact external data they need to answer questions accurately without hallucinating.

cards.title: The Kitchen Assembly Line
cards.body: Think of the full RAG stack like running a high-end restaurant kitchen. Chunking and embeddings are how you prep and label ingredients, vector search is the runner finding them in the pantry, reranking is the head chef selecting the best items, and the LLM is the chef plating the final dish.

cards.title: RAG in Action
cards.body: When a user asks 'What is our refund policy?', the system chunks the policy doc, matches it using vector search, reranks the best paragraphs, fetches live account data via MCP, and prompts the LLM to write a personalized reply.

cards.title: The Model Isn't Everything
cards.body: Many believe that building a great LLM app just requires a smart model. In reality, a model is only as good as the pipeline delivering its context; bad chunking or missing retrieval protocols like MCP will break the system.

cards.title: Static Knowledge vs. Dynamic Stack
cards.body: See how basic LLMs rely only on frozen knowledge, while a Full-Stack RAG application uses live, structured data pathways.

left.text: Relies on pre-trained memory. Can hallucinate when asked about private or live data.

right.text: Retrieves fresh chunks, reranks them, and uses MCP to pull live data before answering.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary role of reranking in a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline?

options.text: To split text into smaller paragraphs
To re-order retrieved documents so the most relevant ones are presented first
To translate text into numerical vector representations
To compress the prompt to save token costs

exercises.explain: Reranking acts as a secondary filter, scoring retrieved documents to ensure the absolute most relevant context is placed at the top of the prompt for the LLM.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Raw documents must be broken down (chunked) before generating embeddings to ensure the search retrieves precise, relevant paragraphs.
exercises.explain: Chunking is crucial because LLMs have a limited context window and process smaller, focused paragraphs much more accurately than massive, unfiltered documents.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing protocol that connects the LLM securely to your files and tools.
exercises.sentence: We use the Model Context Protocol to securely connect our AI app to local files and API tools.
exercises.explain: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) acts as the bridge, allowing LLMs to securely query external tools and data sources.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following sounds like it was written by an AI buzzword generator instead of a real software engineer?

options.text: I configured our chunking strategy to use 500-token blocks and connected it to a Pinecone vector index.
I implemented an MCP server to let the LLM execute SQL queries directly on our database.
I leveraged high-dimensional synergistic optimization to seamlessly align our embeddings with neural networks.

exercises.explain: Option C contains generic AI-style jargon describing a workflow without concrete engineering substance, whereas A and B describe valid developer steps.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you are building a production-ready QA bot. What is the correct sequence of operations to process raw document files and return a highly accurate, context-aware answer to a user query?

options.text: Chunking -> Embeddings -> Vector Search -> Reranking -> LLM Generation
LLM Generation -> Chunking -> Vector Search -> Reranking -> Embeddings
Reranking -> Embeddings -> Chunking -> MCP Retrieval -> LLM Generation
Vector Search -> Chunking -> Embeddings -> Reranking -> Prompting

exercises.explain: Every stage in the RAG pipeline serves a specific purpose: chunking breaks down data, embeddings and vector search locate relevant segments, reranking ensures the highest quality context is positioned first, and MCP connects it all to external data tools so the LLM can generate an accurate response.
